summary: The rewritten staged load harness now completes and writes artifacts, but the real load pass still fails at the bulk student join stage.

details.latestFailure: The July 12, 2026 controlled run completed with 100% socket connection success, but both the 100-user and 500-user runs timed out waiting for `exam:joined` for every client.
details.observedBottleneck: The active bottleneck is bulk `student:join_exam` fan-in under load. Downstream phases never started because joins did not complete within the controlled window.
notes: The harness was rewritten into setup/connect/join/start/save/heartbeat/submit/cleanup phases.
Fixtures were made idempotent and 100/500 user datasets were separated.
Socket authentication fan-in was reduced by direct JWT signing.
Redis startup was healthy in strict redis-only mode during the load pass.
A single-client probe still completes `student:join_exam`, so the blocker is load-path fan-in rather than basic socket auth.
No trustworthy readiness claim is made for 100 or 500 concurrent students.
